body.title: agent_datacenter: Claude device — MCP-native registration, CC.0 and CC.N mailboxes
body.result: ClaudeDevice + ClaudeShim + ygm_check.py shipped; UserPromptSubmit hook + JSONL fallback
body.description:
Register Claude as a rack device via MCP natively. Claude's device is unique: it registers through the MCP connection, not via subprocess.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/devices/claude/device.py:
  class ClaudeDevice(BaseDevice):
    who_am_i() → {'name': 'claude', 'mailbox': current_mailbox(), 'session': session_id_or_global}
    capabilities() → {'ygm': True, 'stop': True, 'nudge': True, 'read_write': True}
    comms() → {'nudge': True} — only device with Nudge flag
    health() → {'mcp_connected': bool, 'mailbox': mailbox_name, 'unseen_count': int}
    Mailboxes: CC.0 (global) + CC.{session_id} if CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is set
    Uses constants from devices/claude/constants.py (T-adc-claude-mailbox-naming-lock)
    Registration: Claude registers itself on session start by calling rack.register (via MCP)
    Shim: ClaudeShim handles the MCP handshake; 'restart' for Claude = re-establish MCP session

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/claude/device.py
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/claude/shim.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: ClaudeDevice class; capabilities() explicitly marks Nudge=True
- docs-in-code: module docstring explains why Claude is the only Nudge device (query pipeline hook)

Scope boundary:
IN: ClaudeDevice + ClaudeShim, mailbox registration via MCP, CC.0 + CC.N naming
OUT: YGM pipeline implementation (T-adc-ygm-nudge-pipeline), session scoping details (handled by constants.py)

Test plan:
- ClaudeDevice() instantiates (all abstract methods implemented)
- capabilities()['nudge'] == True
- health() returns mailbox name = CC.0 when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set
- health() returns mailbox name = CC.{id} when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is set
- ClaudeDevice registers on Skeleton; appears in rack.devices()
